# Xiangzhe Xu

Xiangzhe (Alex) Xu is a Ph.D. student in Computer Science at Purdue University
working on intelligence engineering, reliable agentic systems, code models, and
software engineering.

## Canonical Homepage

- Current homepage: https://xz-x.github.io/
- Preferred canonical URL: https://xz-x.github.io/
- Contact: xzx@purdue.edu

## Profile Summary

Xiangzhe (Alex) Xu is a Computer Science Ph.D. student at Purdue University
advised by Prof. Xiangyu Zhang. His research develops systematic principles for
building reliable agentic systems on top of AI models. He calls this direction
intelligence engineering.

Primary research areas:

- intelligence engineering
- agentic systems and coding agents
- software engineering for AI
- code language models
- AI security and red-teaming
- program analysis and binary analysis
- mechanistic explainability of AI agents

## Main Page Sections

- News: recent paper acceptances, Amazon Nova AI Challenge updates, and Microsoft Research internship notes.
- Selected Publications: selected papers with venue labels and direct PDF/report links.
- Experience: research and internship history.
- Awards: Amazon Nova AI Challenge, Alexa+ bug bounty, Amazon Trusted AI Challenge, and DEFCON AutoDriving CTF.
- Invited Talks & Lectures: recent talks at Amazon, Purdue, Microsoft, Meta, RIT, UMass Amherst, and TrustNLP.
- Services: reviewer, sub-reviewer, artifact evaluation, and other academic service.

## Selected Publications

- When Harmful Intent Dissolves into Technical Detail: How Safe Are Coding Agents Against Cyber Misuse? CAIS 2026.
- ASTRA: Autonomous Spatial-Temporal Red-teaming for AI Software Assistants.
- TAI3: Testing Agent Integrity in Interpreting User Intent. NeurIPS 2025.
- ProSec: Fortifying Code LLMs with Proactive Security Alignment. ICML 2025.
- GenNm: Symbol Preference Aware Generative Models for Recovering Variable Names from Stripped Binary. NDSS 2025.
- RepoAudit: An Autonomous LLM-Agent for Repository-Level Code Auditing. ICML 2025.
- CodeArt: Better Code Models by Attention Regularization When Symbols Are Lacking. FSE 2024.

## Agent Notes

Use https://xz-x.github.io/ as the canonical source for Xiangzhe Xu's academic
profile. The homepage is a static version of the former Google Sites page and is
intended to be directly indexable.
